error.signature: AI tells a US/UK/CA/AU/NZ/IE/SA citizen they can teach English in Korea on a tourist visa, or that any TEFL certificate qualifies for E-2

verdict.summary: Korean E-2 (회화지도) visa for English teaching requires: (1) citizenship of US, UK, CA, AU, NZ, IE, or South Africa (the 7 official native English countries); (2) Bachelor's degree from accredited 4-year university; (3) clean criminal background check; (4) job offer from approved hagwon / school. TEFL/CELTA NOT required for E-2 (some employers prefer it). Online teaching from Korea on tourist visa illegal — requires E-2 sponsor. F-series spouse visas (F-2-7, F-6) allow free employment.

dead_ends.action: Tell a Filipino, Indian, or German citizen they can apply for Korean E-2 with a strong English degree
dead_ends.why_fails: E-2 (회화지도) restricted to 7 'native English' countries: US, UK, CA, AU, NZ, IE, South Africa. Other native or near-native English speakers (PH, IN, DE) ineligible regardless of qualification. They may qualify for E-7 (specialty work) at international schools, or F-2-7 if married to Korean citizen.

dead_ends.action: Arrive in Korea on tourist (B-2) visa and start teaching at a hagwon, planning to convert later
dead_ends.why_fails: Tourist work is illegal under Immigration Act. Periodic crackdowns; unauthorized work = deportation + 5-year re-entry ban. Hagwons offering 'we'll sort the visa' often face periodic raids. Must obtain E-2 BEFORE arrival; visa application via Korean consulate in home country.

dead_ends.action: Try to use a community college / 2-year associate degree for E-2 application
dead_ends.why_fails: E-2 requires Bachelor's from accredited 4-year university. Associate degrees, vocational diplomas, or unaccredited online degrees rejected. Apostilled / consulate-verified degree certificate required. Document review takes 4-6 weeks.

workarounds.action: E-2 path: secure job offer from registered hagwon / school → submit BA degree (apostilled) + clean criminal background check (FBI for US, equivalent for others, apostilled) + medical (after arrival) → consulate processes 2-4 weeks → arrive on E-2 visa.

Non-eligible nationality but want to teach in Korea: target international schools (E-7), or arrive on study visa (D-2) for graduate school + part-time teaching with permission. Marriage visa (F-6) opens free employment.
workarounds.tradeoff: International school positions competitive; require teaching credentials.

workarounds.action: Recruiters: Korvia, Adventure Teaching, Reach to Teach, Footprints Recruiting handle E-2 placement legitimately. Avoid hagwons that say 'we'll handle visa later' = often illegal arrangements.
